title: Durga Puja
intro: Durga Puja is one of the most important and grand Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in West Bengal, Assam, Odisha, Tripura, and many other parts of India.
description:
Durga Puja is one of the most important and grand Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in West Bengal, Assam, Odisha, Tripura, and many other parts of India. The festival celebrates the victory of Goddess Durga over the demon Mahishasura, symbolizing the victory of good over evil. Durga Puja is observed during the Shukla Paksha of the Hindu month of Ashwin, usually in September or October.

According to Hindu traditions, Goddess Durga fought and defeated the powerful demon Mahishasura, who had created fear and suffering in the world. The victory of Maa Durga represents the triumph of divine power, righteousness, and truth over negativity and injustice. During this festival, devotees worship Goddess Durga and seek her blessings for strength, protection, happiness, and prosperity.

Durga Puja is celebrated with beautifully decorated pandals, artistic idols of Maa Durga, devotional prayers, Aarti, cultural programs, and community gatherings. The five main days of celebration begin from Maha Shashti and continue until Vijayadashami, making it one of the most significant cultural and religious festivals of Bengal.

Festival Month

Ashwin (September – October)

Significance / Importance

Durga Puja holds great religious, spiritual, and cultural importance in Hinduism. The festival represents divine feminine power (Shakti) and the victory of righteousness over evil. Goddess Durga is worshipped as the embodiment of strength, protection, courage, and motherhood.

Devotees believe that worshipping Maa Durga removes fear, negativity, and obstacles while bringing courage, peace, happiness, and prosperity into life. The festival inspires people to follow the path of truth, faith, and righteousness.

Puja Vidhi

1. Clean and decorate the home and पूजा area.
2. Install the idol or image of Maa Durga at an auspicious time.
3. Perform Kalash Sthapana and invoke Goddess Durga.
4. Offer flowers, fruits, incense, lamps, clothes, and Naivedya.
5. Recite Durga Saptashati and Durga Mantras.
6. Perform Maa Durga Aarti with devotion.
7. Perform Kanya Puja and distribute Prasad.
8. Pray to Goddess Durga for strength, protection, peace, and prosperity.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Durga Puja Katha
* Mahishasura Vadh Katha
* Durga Saptashati
* Durga Mantra
* Durga Aarti
* Durga Chalisa
* Navratri Katha

faq.a1: Durga Puja is celebrated during the Shukla Paksha of the Hindu month of Ashwin, usually in September or October.
faq.a2: Durga Puja is celebrated to commemorate Goddess Durga’s victory over the demon Mahishasura and the victory of good over evil.
faq.q3: Where is Durga Puja mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: Durga Puja is mainly celebrated in West Bengal, Assam, Odisha, and Tripura.
faq.q4: How many days is Durga Puja celebrated?
faq.a4: The main celebration lasts for five days, from Maha Shashti to Vijayadashami.
faq.q5: Which deity is worshipped during Durga Puja?
faq.a5: Goddess Durga is worshipped during this festival.
faq.a6: Mahishasura was a powerful demon who was defeated by Goddess Durga to restore peace and righteousness.
faq.q7: Why are pandals made during Durga Puja?
faq.a7: Pandals are created to install Maa Durga’s idol and provide a place for worship and community celebrations.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Durga Puja?
faq.a8: Durga Puja symbolizes divine power, courage, devotion, protection, and the victory of good over evil.
